title: [Feature]: Evaluate distributed SQL database sinks
problem: Oracle GoldenGate connectivity material lists distributed SQL and PostgreSQL/MySQL-compatible systems such as SingleStoreDB, YugabyteDB, Greenplum, EDB Postgres Advanced Server, and cloud derivatives. nats-sinks does not yet define how these should relate to MySQL, Postgres, or dedicated sink families.
proposal: Research distributed SQL sink coverage as a family. The evaluation should decide whether systems such as SingleStoreDB, YugabyteDB, Greenplum, and EDB Postgres Advanced Server are certified profiles under future MySQL/Postgres sinks or separate sinks with their own SQL dialect and idempotency tests.
users: Operators of distributed SQL systems who need event persistence with clear duplicate and transaction semantics.
delivery_semantics: ACK-gated success requires committed writes under the selected system's consistency model. Any eventually consistent or asynchronous ingest path must document what durable acceptance means.
security: Credentials, connection strings, schemas, payloads, and query details must be redacted. Parameterized SQL and strict identifier validation are mandatory.
acceptance: Research maps each candidate to a dedicated sink or compatibility profile.
Dialect differences and upsert behavior are documented.
Separate feature requests are created for any accepted dedicated sink.
No implementation starts before certification strategy exists.
tests: Research-only initially. Future profiles need dialect tests, driver fakes, and gated live tests.
documentation: Add distributed SQL evaluation to sink roadmap documentation.
closeout: Close after the evaluation release is published.
